title: Talking About 24 hours of Moab Solo Mountain Bike Race 2010 - 1stx 4/4

transcript.text: Okay this is part four of my 24-hour race there was some stuff I didn't have time to say so I
Do have a part four um okay so I ended at the last part of the race but I just wanted to talk
About like equipment um yeah there's the single speed aspect of it which is really interesting um
When I first found out about single speeds a couple weeks ago you know some guy was like yeah
They're cool the guy was hanging out with it's like yeah they're cool you know they make you
Better rider because you have to spin and then you have to crank and and they're a lot lighter and
Like you know like in one race the pro did normal did 15 laps and pro single speed did 14 so they're
Really not that slower and I was like what really that's weird I don't understand but then I understood
Them after that race because like for me like with four laps to go or something like that
Still a bunch of hours left um like my derailleur wouldn't go back down the halfway down the rear
Cog and I got that real bike like in perfect condition and everything you know and my brother
Was putting oil and stuff in it so just after all that riding it gets so caked you know and by then
I mean if you can't get down that many cogs I could sometimes if I went up to the top and I just used
Momentum to get it down but if you can't get down the past and halfway down your cog you're gonna be
About in the same situation as a single speed guy anyway and only you're gonna be hauling around
A lot more weight at least for the downhill part and also if you're doing like like for 24 hour race
You could be good cool because you're so tired that you want to rest and going downhill it's a good
Time to rest and you wouldn't want to pedal anyway and by the time you you it's your time you want to
Start pedaling again the single speed you're in is the perfect gear and then um and then you can get
Out of the saddle and crank um I didn't see any single speeds that were full suspension I think
That wouldn't work at all because you have to get out of the saddle a lot um but yeah I think there
Were like four girl soloist single speeds and like four girl normal so out of eight girls doing it
Solo like half of nine single speed so and like if you don't have multiple bikes you know it could
Be a good idea because you don't have to worry about the mechanical stuff uh so I kind of understood
It more I'm never going to do that though because I can't crank I I'd blow out my knees if I tried that
That's I understood it more after after experiencing that race um and um yeah I kind of you definitely
Want to know your gear I on my light I had it on upside down so it kept falling off it felt like
Off four three times in one lap and then like four times on another lap and I was like looking at it
Like oh my god my race is ruined it won't stay on in more than a couple of seconds fuck and then I go
Wait just put it on the the right side of the of the freaking stem and then the gravity will hold it
On that's the way it's supposed to go because I was thinking man it's supposed night rider it's
Supposed to be a good lock like how could they design it to fall off like this so I put it on
The other side and it didn't fall off so that was kind of stupid of me um yeah I should have known that
More um and um but yeah 29 inch wheels are way better than those small 26 inch wheels but those
26 inch wheels when I was doing that my that lap before my bike cracked I would like get stuck in
A rock I'd like ride over it and then I'd be like and it's really exhausting to have to like push your
Bike over you know with those big 29 inch wheels they just go and they just bounce over it and uh
Yeah they're way better I was like I felt like I was in like nirvana so I guess they're saying like
I think that's about it yeah okay I'm gonna do it next year too so I'll make a video
